summary: When verifying your own prior claim (Slack reply, ADR, assertion), dispatch an adversarial reviewer AND an evaluator in parallel. One lens alone misses either the conceptual or the methodology error class.
root_cause: A single reviewer focuses either on conceptual soundness OR methodology — not both. Self-verification fails because the verifier shares the same frame as the original claimant.
fix: Dispatch socrates-contrarian (concept attack) and evaluator (methodology check) in parallel. Wait for both receipts before accepting the claim as verified.

summary: ArgoCD access requires three planes: AAD group membership + Enterprise App assignment (groupMembershipClaims:ApplicationGroup) + AppProject role binding. Any missing plane = silent denial.
root_cause: ArgoCD RBAC has three independent permission planes. AAD membership alone is insufficient. The Enterprise App must have groupMembershipClaims:ApplicationGroup set, AND the AppProject must grant the matching role. Missing any one produces silent denial, not an error.
fix: Check all three planes in sequence: (1) az ad group member check; (2) verify Enterprise App groupMembershipClaims setting; (3) kubectl get appproject and check roleBindings. Fix whichever plane is missing.

summary: ADO stage with trigger:none + approval gate that times out → stage silently skips. The 2-hour-exact delta in Timeline Checkpoint.Approval records distinguishes timeout (ADO default) from 'plan no change'. Read Timeline records, not just pipeline logs.
root_cause: ADO's default approval timeout is 2 hours. When trigger:none prevents automatic queuing and the approval window expires, the stage is marked skipped — not failed. Pipeline logs show 'skipped', not 'timed out', creating ambiguity.
fix: Read the build Timeline API records and check Checkpoint.Approval delta. 2h-exact delta = ADO default approval timeout. Distinguish from plan-no-change skip by checking if Terraform ran at all.

summary: Rootly resolve does NOT close upstream Azure Monitor alerts. RCA action steps must explicitly drive both Rootly and Microsoft.AlertsManagement/alerts/<id> to terminal state. Resolving only one leaves a zombie alert.
root_cause: Rootly and Azure Monitor alerts are independent systems. Rootly resolve is an incident management action; it does not call the Azure API to fire off alert resolution. Azure Monitor alerts remain active until explicitly resolved via az monitor or REST API.
fix: After resolving in Rootly, also run: az rest --method POST --url '/subscriptions/{sub}/providers/Microsoft.AlertsManagement/alerts/{id}/changestate?api-version=2019-05-05-preview&newState=Closed'. Confirm both planes show terminal state.

summary: CCoE Key Vault module hardcodes ServiceApiLatency Avg>1000ms alert — fires on low-volume bootstrap KVs ~every 2-3.5 weeks across all products. This alert class is structurally noisy, not operationally actionable.
root_cause: CCoE baseline module has a hardcoded alert threshold that is not tuned for bootstrap/low-volume Key Vaults. Bootstrap KVs have sporadic request patterns that naturally exceed the latency threshold.
fix: On receipt of CCoE KeyVault latency alert on a bootstrap KV: verify it is a bootstrap KV (low traffic expected), verify no active operations in flight, close as expected-noise. Do not escalate or page unless concurrent with a real incident.

summary: Credential expiry is a recurring CLASS problem at Trade Platform (5 incidents in 18 months: INC-75 AAD SP 2024-11-19; F4 AAD SP 2025-12-29; PXQ KV 2026-05-07; argo-cd-sandbox PAT 2026-05-11; latent 2026-06-01 3 MC PATs). Per-incident rotation does NOT reduce class recurrence. Class-level remediation mandatory: eliminate calendar-expiring credentials (Workload Identity Federation) OR automate rotation (KV + ESO + scheduler). Standing rule: any PR introducing a new credential MUST declare rotation owner, verification path, alarm surface in the same PR.
root_cause: Manual oral procedure carried by one engineer (Fabrizio Zavalloni); no class-level structural fix has shipped despite multiple post-incident promises since 2024-11 (INC-75). Each instance is firefought as if novel.
fix: Phase 1 (now-30d, unconditional): SLA + Grafana alert (argocd_appset_status{condition_type='ErrorOccurred'} > 0) + named ownership rotation. Phase 2 (30-180d): choose Workload Identity Federation OR KV+ESO scheduled rotation based on Fabrizio's gap-list answers in how-to-rotate.md §7. Phase 3 (180d+): extend to F4 AAD SP, ESP cert, TF SP, BTM, Snyk classes.

summary: Azure scheduled-query-rule property autoMitigate=false is ORTHOGONAL to severity. A sev-2 rule with autoMitigate=false has the same 'stays Fired forever' property as a sev-0; severity changes who-gets-paged-how, not auto-close behavior. Any paging-bound rule with autoMitigate=false MUST have: documented manual-close runbook, action group routing to 24/7 surface, runbook URL in rule description. Sev-0 + autoMitigate=false requires extra reviewer.
root_cause: Conflating severity and autoMitigate at design time leads to over-broad sev-0 rules that fire indefinitely on transient triggers (today: CMC vpp-resource-unhealthy fired on Microsoft 5Z1B-6KG late-ingestion).
fix: Add autoMitigate audit to alert-authoring checklist: quarterly query `az monitor scheduled-query list --query "[?autoMitigate==\`false\`].{name:name, sev:severity}"`; each result must have manual-close protocol or justified exception. Block sev-0 + autoMitigate=false in CI gate.

summary: Azure Monitor V2 scheduled-query-rules evaluate windows by ingestion_time(), NOT TimeGenerated. During Microsoft platform latency incidents (5Z1B-6KG: Log Analytics + Application Insights West Europe latency 2026-05-11), workspace rows can land minutes-to-hours after emission. Engine's built-in late-data-settling period delays evaluation; late-arrived rows fall into window and trigger fire. Alerts can fire 20+ min AFTER an event's nominal TimeGenerated. Alert KQL MUST narrow CategoryValue predicates; raw 'ServiceHealth' matches Microsoft's full announcement stream.
root_cause: Out-of-IaC manually-created rule with single-predicate KQL had no narrowing filter; late-ingestion mechanism + Microsoft platform incident combined to fire on stale data.
fix: Pre-deploy adversarial review on any new Azure scheduled-query rule MUST include backtest against a known prior Microsoft platform incident. Prefer 'ResourceHealth + Activated/action + ResourceProviderValue' over raw 'ServiceHealth' for resource-centric semantics. Add ingestion-latency monitoring on workspaces in regions with historical platform incidents (West Europe).

summary: When an incident's RCA must choose between multiple uneliminated causal hypotheses, the correct RCA shape is observation-only — install discriminator probes + reader's mental model; ship NO fix recommendation. Today's CPU throttling RCA (otc-container, eneco-vpp namespace, dev cluster) carried 4 uneliminated hypotheses (H-A undersized CPU, H-B memory pressure upstream, H-C rule mis-calibrated for sidecar class, H-D debug exporter verbose); each implies a different fix. Picking one without elimination ships the wrong fix.
root_cause: Hypothesis dependency is non-trivial (H-A is symptom; H-B/H-D are candidate upstream causes; H-C is orthogonal); a snapshot decision ignores dependency structure.
fix: Document the observation-only L8 template in the RCA process. Adjudication heuristic: run all hypothesis-cheapest-probes; if H-D-class confirms (cheapest+most-reversible), ship that fix first. If H-B-class confirms with H-A-class together, do NOT fix the symptom until the upstream is understood.

summary: At Eneco Trade Platform, on-call incidents land in 4+ different intake channels per shift: #myriad-platform (public, bot-driven cards), #team-platform (private internal triage), Rootly direct page (alertmanager-routed; no Slack mention), ServiceNow CMC ticket (sometimes pasted into Slack as URL, often not). Today's 2026-05-11 shift had FOUR incidents and EACH was in a different channel. Reading only #myriad-platform misses 50%+ of an on-call's actual work. Future summarizers MUST sample ALL surfaces.
root_cause: Multiple alert/intake systems wire to different end-state surfaces with no canonical aggregator. Each surface has its own ownership and notification pattern. Coordination implicit on humans, not on a single dashboard.
fix: On-call summary protocol: enumerate ALL surfaces (Slack public + private + Rootly + SN + RCA dirs) before composing the shift report. Use eneco-context-slack for Slack surfaces, eneco-tools-rootly for Rootly, manual ServiceNow check. Cross-correlate by time window.

summary: FBE slot recycling can leave Azure resources orphaned in rg-vpp-app-sb-401 (Sandbox sub 7b1ba02e-...) that are NOT tracked in the slot's Terraform state. Three uneliminated provenance paths: (P1) failed destroy with terraform state rm workaround; (P2) out-of-band create; (P3) Terraform version drift (1.14.3 create vs 1.13.1 destroy → silent skip on state-version-mismatch). Apply-time error: azurerm_eventhub_namespace 'vpp-evh-premium-<slot>' already exists - needs to be imported into State. Fix is delete-recreate (when orphan is empty); destroy pipeline MUST verify zero residue before slot release.
root_cause: Destroy pipeline azure-pipeline-fbe-del.yml lacks residue-zero check; create/destroy pipelines on misaligned Terraform versions (F19).
fix: Add residue-zero check to destroy pipeline (per slot, query az resource list -g rg-vpp-app-sb-401 for resources matching slot name; fail if non-zero). Align Terraform versions between create (1.14.3) and destroy (1.13.1). Audit all 10 slots for existing orphans.

summary: Alerts created manually outside IaC during platform stand-up never get adopted into IaC; their KQL/thresholds age silently because no review surface owns them. Today's CMC alert rule was created 2024-01-24 by Conclusion vendor identity eelke.hoffman@conclusion.nl; single-predicate KQL; sev-0; autoMitigate=false; actions=null — escaped IaC review for 15.5 months until firing on Microsoft 5Z1B-6KG-driven late ingestion. Defense: quarterly inventory diff (az monitor scheduled-query list per subscription vs IaC-derived expected name list); any unmatched rule is adopt-or-delete within one sprint.
root_cause: No review surface; no quarterly audit cadence; vendor-identity alert authoring left no IaC trace.
fix: Quarterly inventory diff job (CI or scheduled task); alert-authoring checklist requires PR-only rule creation (no portal authoring on prd); CI gate refuses actions=null. Vendor-identity governance: should vendors create sev-0 paging rules on prd subscription? Surface to SRE/Platform leadership.

summary: SBSM (servicebus-subscriptions-manager) has THREE CD pipeline patterns, not the two its docs describe: (1) cd-myriad-platform (8574) auto-deploys on myriad-platform.yaml + infra/terragrunt/** changes; (2) cd-<consumer-team> per-contract auto-deploy pipelines exist for major consumers (e.g. cd-flex-trade-optimizer = 8995) and auto-trigger on their own contract yaml + infra/terragrunt/**/subscription_creator/** changes with NO approval gates; (3) cd-general (8573) is manual-trigger fallback for contracts WITHOUT a dedicated pipeline (used today only by asset-scheduling). Repo docs (docs/internals/cicd/cd-ado-pipeline-configuration.md) describe only patterns 1 and 3 — pattern 2 is undocumented and a likely source of incorrect on-call diagnoses. Pipeline-success != contract-realization: a Terragrunt apply --auto-approve can exit 0 while one of N for_each-keyed resources is silently absent from Azure; only an externally-witnessable parity probe (yaml entries vs Azure resources per env) proves full realization.
root_cause: Three independent issues: (a) docs/internals/cicd/cd-ado-pipeline-configuration.md is stale and lists only cd-myriad-platform and cd-general; (b) per-contract auto-deploy pipelines are registered via just ado-pipelines-register without doc updates; (c) Terragrunt apply --auto-approve --non-interactive can return success codes despite a single-resource silent skip whose root mechanism is uncharacterized (likely provider-level partial failure on parallel for_each resource creation).
fix: On-call triage for 'subscription missing on SBSM-managed contract': (1) az servicebus topic show — falsify topic-missing; (2) grep contract yaml for (topic_name, subscription_name, environment_name) tuple — falsify contract-missing; (3) az pipelines list --query for cd-<consumer-team> to identify the actual deployment pipeline (do NOT assume cd-general); (4) parity probe ALL subs for same consumer+env — 1/N missing localises to single-resource drift; 0/N localises to pipeline never ran; (5) MANDATORY gate-zero before any pipeline trigger: terragrunt state list against the affected env backend — state-has-resource branches to terragrunt state rm + re-apply (no-op apply trap), state-lacks-resource branches to pipeline re-trigger. References RCA at log/employer/eneco/02_on_call_shift/2026_05_12_topic_not_found/rca.md with full evidence chain.

summary: Adversarial subagent dispatch MUST include explicit 'Write your receipt to <absolute-path>, return only the file path' instruction; coordinator MUST run `test -s` on each artifact before consuming the agent's output. Brain rule NN-2 mandates that subagent artifacts are always written to disk. Brain rule 'Delegation discipline: >200 prescriptive tokens = agent-as-file-writer' applies to nearly all adversarial dispatches (Sherlock/Socrates/El-Demoledor prompts are typically 400-600 tokens). Returning prose in task notifications is NOT compliant — the receipt only lives in conversation history and is invisible to future sessions, audit, and lessons-learned harvesting.
root_cause: Tone-bypass — user framings like 'quick check first', 'just take a look', 'wdyt' relax coordinator discipline despite brain explicitly stating 'tone is signal, never exemption'. The relaxation manifests as: skipping manifest.json init, skipping P1 preflight mirror to disk, skipping artifact-write instructions in subagent prompts, skipping post-dispatch `test -s` verification. Real incident: 2026-05-12 FTO strike-price RCA had three adversarial reviewers (sherlock, socrates, el-demoledor) return prose to task notifications; zero files written by agents; coordinator detected the violation only when user asked directly. Receipts had to be reconstructed verbatim post-hoc into .ai/tasks/2026-05-12-001_topic-not-found/adversarial/*.md.
fix: Mandatory dispatch template for adversarial subagents: prompt MUST end with 'Write your full receipt as a markdown file to <absolute-path>/<reviewer-name>.md including frontmatter (task_id, agent, status, summary, date). Return ONLY the file path on completion. The coordinator will Read the file.' Coordinator post-dispatch MUST: (1) wait for completion notification; (2) run `test -s <path>` to verify non-empty file; (3) Read the file to consume the receipt; (4) update manifest.json adversarial_receipts[] with the path. Pre-flight rule: when emitting GATE-CLEARED, also create manifest.json with task_id/slug/task_root/external_writes/adversarial_receipts/gate_witnesses fields. The 'quick check first' / 'simple task' framing does NOT downgrade these mandates.

summary: Azure AD AADSTS650057 on `az` CLI against an Eneco custom API is structurally ambiguous: the error blames the client's app registration, but the load-bearing missing entity is almost always on the resource side or in a separate runtime directory object. Three gates control whether AAD will issue a delegated token: (1) `resource.api.preAuthorizedApplications` includes the client appId; (2) tenant-wide `oauth2PermissionGrant` (consentType=AllPrincipals) exists for client+resource+scope; (3) per-user `oauth2PermissionGrant` (consentType=Principal) exists for client+resource+scope+user. Group membership (`appRoleAssignedTo` for a group) and `appRoleAssignmentRequired` are DIFFERENT gates that affect what the token CONTAINS, not whether AAD issues one. Eneco BTM convention does not use `preAuthorizedApplications`, so new joiners hit (3)-only paths and fail until they consent once via `az login --scope api://<resource>/<scope>`.
root_cause: BTM platform-AAD convention relies on per-user consent rather than admin pre-authorization. Per-user consent grants are runtime directory objects invisible to Terraform; they drift independently of IaC. Each new joiner must complete the interactive consent step manually; no automation, no onboarding doc captures this. PR 172140 ('Fix not being able to get tokens from public clients (bruno)') added Bruno redirect URIs but did NOT add Azure CLI to preAuthorizedApplications, so anyone reading the commit assumes az CLI now works tenant-wide — it does not.
fix: Triage in three commands: (a) `az ad app show --id <api-guid> --query api.preAuthorizedApplications`; (b) `az rest GET /v1.0/oauth2PermissionGrants?$filter=clientId eq '<client-sp-id>' and resourceId eq '<resource-sp-id>' and consentType eq 'AllPrincipals'`; (c) same filter with `principalId eq '<user-oid>'`. The first non-empty result names the gate. If all empty, run `az login --scope api://<api-guid>/<scope>` interactively to create the per-user grant. Durable fix options: admin-grant tenant-wide consent (`az ad app permission grant --id <client> --api <resource> --scope <scope>`) or declare `pre_authorized_applications` in IaC (changes BTM convention; needs platform-AAD sign-off and security review).
